seoTitle: Aowei Supercapacitors | Cylindrical, Prismatic, Modules & Hybrid Capacitors
seoDescription: Aowei supercapacitor product catalog: cylindrical EDLC, prismatic cells, module systems, and hybrid capacitors. Technical specifications, selection guide, and distributor pricing.

faqs.question: What types of supercapacitors does Aowei manufacture?
faqs.answer: Aowei manufactures four main categories of supercapacitors. Cylindrical supercapacitors are their standard EDLC cells ranging from 1F to 3000F, featuring radial and axial lead configurations for through-hole mounting. Prismatic supercapacitors offer compact rectangular form factors ideal for space-constrained applications. Module systems integrate multiple cells with active balancing circuits and thermal management for high-voltage applications. Hybrid capacitors combine lithium-ion technology with EDLC construction, delivering higher energy density while maintaining high power density characteristics. Each product line is optimized for specific applications from consumer electronics to industrial energy storage systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below to find detailed specifications. Contact our FAE team with your application requirements for personalized product recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I choose between cylindrical and prismatic supercapacitors?
faqs.answer: The choice between cylindrical and prismatic supercapacitors depends on your application's mechanical and electrical requirements. Cylindrical cells typically offer higher capacitance values per unit volume and are well-suited for applications requiring individual cell replacement or standard through-hole mounting. They excel in automotive, industrial, and energy storage applications where robust construction and proven reliability are critical. Prismatic cells provide better volumetric efficiency for PCB mounting and are ideal for portable electronics, IoT devices, and applications with height constraints. Prismatic designs also enable more efficient thermal management in compact enclosures. Consider your available mounting space, thermal environment, required capacitance, and manufacturing assembly process when making your selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the dimensional drawings in each product category. Request samples of both form factors for evaluation in your prototype if space constraints are critical.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of Aowei hybrid capacitors over standard EDLC?
faqs.answer: Aowei hybrid capacitors, also known as lithium-ion capacitors (LIC), offer significant advantages over standard EDLC supercapacitors in specific applications. Hybrid capacitors achieve 3-5 times higher energy density (up to 30 Wh/kg) compared to EDLC cells, enabling longer backup times and reduced system size. They maintain excellent power density characteristics, capable of rapid charge and discharge cycles. The hybrid design uses a pre-doped lithium-ion anode combined with an activated carbon cathode, resulting in higher nominal voltage (3.8V vs 2.7V) and improved energy storage capacity. These characteristics make hybrid capacitors ideal for applications requiring both high energy and high power, such as wearable devices, wireless sensors, and portable medical equipment. However, they have lower cycle life (50,000-100,000 cycles) compared to EDLC (500,000+ cycles), so application requirements must be carefully evaluated.
faqs.decisionGuide: If your application requires backup times exceeding 60 seconds or has strict size constraints, hybrid capacitors may be the optimal choice. Contact our FAE team for detailed comparison analysis.

faqs.question: When should I use a supercapacitor module instead of individual cells?
faqs.answer: Supercapacitor modules are recommended when your application requires operating voltages exceeding the 2.7-3.8V rating of individual cells, or when you need a complete energy storage solution with integrated management. Modules series-connect multiple cells to achieve higher voltages (16V to hundreds of volts) while incorporating active balancing circuits to ensure voltage equalization across cells. This prevents overvoltage conditions that could damage individual cells and extends overall system life. Modules also include thermal management features, monitoring capabilities, and standardized electrical connections that simplify system integration. Applications such as electric vehicle regenerative braking, grid-scale energy storage, and industrial UPS systems typically require module configurations. The additional cost of module integration is offset by reduced engineering effort, improved reliability, and faster time-to-market.
faqs.decisionGuide: If your system voltage exceeds 15V or you need more than 4 cells in series, a module solution is strongly recommended. Review our module systems category for standard configurations or inquire about custom designs.

faqs.question: What are the key parameters to consider when selecting a supercapacitor?
faqs.answer: Selecting the optimal supercapacitor requires evaluation of several critical parameters. Capacitance determines energy storage capacity and is measured in Farads (F). Rated voltage specifies the maximum operating voltage, typically 2.7V for EDLC and 3.8V for hybrid capacitors. ESR (Equivalent Series Resistance) affects power delivery capability and self-heating during charge/discharge cycles. Leakage current indicates the rate of self-discharge when the capacitor is idle. Operating temperature range defines environmental limits, with Aowei products rated from -40°C to +65°C. Cycle life specifies the number of charge/discharge cycles before capacitance degrades below 80% of initial value. Physical dimensions and mounting style must fit your mechanical constraints. Finally, consider safety certifications required for your application, such as UL recognition for consumer products or AEC-Q200 for automotive.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our online selection tool or contact our FAE team with your voltage, energy, power, and environmental requirements for detailed recommendations.

categories.name: Cylindrical Supercapacitors
categories.description: Aowei's cylindrical supercapacitors represent the industry standard for EDLC technology, offering capacitance values from 1F to 3000F in robust radial and axial lead configurations. These cells utilize high-purity activated carbon electrodes and organic electrolyte systems to deliver exceptional power density, low ESR, and cycle life exceeding 500,000 cycles. The cylindrical construction provides excellent mechanical stability and thermal performance, making these cells ideal for demanding automotive, industrial, and energy storage applications. All cylindrical cells feature laser-welded hermetic seals ensuring long-term reliability and are 100% tested for capacitance, ESR, and leakage current before shipment.

selectionGuide.title: Cylindrical Supercapacitor Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Technical guide for selecting the optimal cylindrical cell based on capacitance, voltage, and application requirements

faqs.question: What is the maximum operating voltage for Aowei cylindrical supercapacitors?
faqs.answer: Aowei cylindrical supercapacitors are rated for 2.7V nominal voltage with a maximum operating voltage of 2.85V. This voltage rating is standard across the EDLC industry and is determined by the electrochemical stability window of the organic electrolyte and activated carbon electrode combination. Operating above 2.85V will accelerate electrolyte decomposition, leading to increased ESR, reduced capacitance, and shortened operational life. For applications requiring higher voltages, cells must be connected in series with appropriate voltage balancing. Aowei recommends maintaining at least 10% voltage derating (operating at 2.43V or below) for applications requiring maximum cycle life, particularly in high-temperature environments above 45°C.
faqs.decisionGuide: For system voltages above 2.7V, consider our module systems with integrated balancing, or contact our FAE team for series connection design guidance.

faqs.question: How does temperature affect cylindrical supercapacitor performance?
faqs.answer: Temperature significantly impacts supercapacitor performance across multiple parameters. At low temperatures (below -20°C), ESR increases substantially due to reduced electrolyte ionic conductivity, limiting high-current discharge capability. Capacitance also decreases slightly at low temperatures, typically by 10-15% at -40°C. At high temperatures (above 45°C), electrolyte degradation accelerates, reducing operational life according to the Arrhenius equation - approximately doubling the degradation rate for every 10°C increase. Leakage current increases exponentially with temperature, affecting long-term energy retention. Aowei cylindrical cells are rated for -40°C to +65°C operation, but optimal performance and lifetime are achieved between -20°C and +45°C. For extreme temperature applications, thermal management including heating or cooling may be necessary.
faqs.decisionGuide: If your application operates outside the -20°C to +45°C range, contact our FAE team for thermal management recommendations and lifetime calculations.

faqs.question: What is the typical cycle life of Aowei cylindrical supercapacitors?
faqs.answer: Aowei cylindrical supercapacitors are rated for 500,000 to 1,000,000 charge/discharge cycles depending on the specific series and operating conditions. This exceptional cycle life is a fundamental advantage of EDLC technology compared to batteries (typically 500-2000 cycles). Cycle life is defined as the number of cycles before capacitance degrades below 80% of initial rated value when operated within specified parameters. Key factors affecting cycle life include operating voltage (lower voltage extends life), operating temperature (lower temperature extends life), and depth of discharge (shallower cycles extend life). At rated voltage and 25°C, Aowei cells maintain 90% of initial capacitance after 100,000 cycles and 80% after 500,000 cycles. For applications requiring maximum longevity, operating at 80% of rated voltage can extend cycle life to over 1,000,000 cycles.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring more than 500,000 cycles, specify the AW-HC (High Cycle) series or contact our FAE team for derating recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the energy storage capacity of a cylindrical supercapacitor?
faqs.answer: The energy storage capacity of a supercapacitor can be calculated using the fundamental equation E = 1/2 * C * V², where E is energy in Joules, C is capacitance in Farads, and V is voltage in Volts. For practical applications, it's often useful to convert to watt-hours (Wh) by dividing Joules by 3600. For example, a 100F, 2.7V supercapacitor stores E = 0.5 * 100 * (2.7)² = 364.5 Joules or approximately 0.1 Wh. When calculating usable energy, consider the minimum operating voltage of your application - energy can only be extracted down to this minimum voltage. The usable energy is therefore E_usable = 1/2 * C * (V_max² - V_min²). For systems with multiple cells in series, use the total capacitance (C_total = C_cell / n for n series cells) and total voltage (V_total = V_cell * n) in the calculation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our online energy calculator or contact our FAE team for detailed sizing calculations including efficiency and voltage balancing losses.

faqs.question: What mounting options are available for cylindrical supercapacitors?
faqs.answer: Aowei cylindrical supercapacitors are available with multiple mounting configurations to suit different assembly requirements. Standard radial lead mounting features two leads extending from the bottom of the cell for through-hole PCB mounting, with lead spacing of 5mm or 7.5mm depending on cell diameter. This is the most common configuration for general-purpose applications. Snap-in terminals provide secure mechanical mounting for larger cells (350F and above) with positive retention in the PCB. Axial lead configurations have leads extending from both ends of the cylindrical cell, suitable for chassis mounting or applications requiring vertical mounting orientation. Some models offer threaded terminal posts for busbar connections in high-current applications. All mounting styles are designed to withstand typical shock and vibration requirements for automotive and industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the mechanical drawings for each part number to confirm mounting compatibility with your PCB layout or chassis design.

products.name: 100F 2.7V Cylindrical Supercapacitor
products.shortDescription: High-performance 100F EDLC cell with low ESR for automotive and industrial pulse power applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The AW-2R7-J107UY is a 100 Farad, 2.7V cylindrical EDLC supercapacitor designed for high-reliability applications requiring rapid charge/discharge cycles and long operational life.
Featuring Aowei's proprietary activated carbon electrode technology and high-purity organic electrolyte, this cell delivers exceptional power density with ESR as low as 18mΩ.
The robust cylindrical aluminum case with laser-welded seal ensures reliable operation in harsh environments with operating temperatures from -40°C to +65°C.
features: Ultra-low ESR for high power density

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Energy Storage Systems
faeReview.content: I've specified the AW-2R7-J107UY in numerous automotive start-stop applications, and it consistently delivers excellent performance. The 18mΩ ESR is genuinely impressive for a 100F cell - I've measured actual samples at 15-16mΩ, which translates to lower self-heating during rapid charge cycles. In one customer application, we replaced a competitor's 120F cell with this 100F Aowei part and actually achieved better system performance due to the lower ESR. The cycle life claims are conservative - we've tested samples beyond 600,000 cycles with less than 15% capacitance degradation. I particularly appreciate the consistent cell-to-cell matching, which is critical when designing series-connected modules. For any automotive or industrial pulse power application, this is my go-to recommendation in the 100F class.
faeReview.highlight: Exceptional ESR performance and consistent cell matching for reliable module designs

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the AW-2R7-J107UY?
faqs.answer: The AW-2R7-J107UY is rated for a maximum ripple current of 8.5A at 25°C and 120Hz frequency. This rating ensures the cell operates within safe thermal limits during continuous charge/discharge cycling. At higher frequencies, the effective ripple current capability may be reduced due to increased ESR heating. For pulsed applications with duty cycles below 50%, higher peak currents up to 15A are permissible for short durations (less than 10 seconds) provided the average power dissipation does not exceed continuous ratings. The ripple current capability decreases at elevated temperatures - approximately 6.5A at 45°C and 4.5A at 65°C. Always verify the actual operating temperature in your application and ensure adequate thermal management for high-ripple applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring continuous ripple current above 8A, consider parallel connection of multiple cells or upgrading to the 220F model with lower ESR.

faqs.question: How long can the AW-2R7-J107UY provide backup power?
faqs.answer: The backup time provided by the AW-2R7-J107UY depends on the load current and allowable voltage drop. The cell stores approximately 0.1 Wh of energy (E = 0.5 * 100F * 2.7V² = 364.5 Joules). For a constant current load, backup time can be calculated using t = C * (V_initial - V_final) / I_load. For example, at 100mA load current from 2.7V to 1.35V (50% voltage drop), backup time is approximately 100F * 1.35V / 0.1A = 1350 seconds or 22.5 minutes. At 1A load, backup time reduces to approximately 2.25 minutes. For power-critical applications, it's recommended to maintain at least 20% voltage margin above the minimum operating voltage of your system. The actual usable energy is therefore E_usable = 0.5 * C * (V_max² - V_min²), which should be used for precise calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate your specific backup requirements using our online calculator or contact our FAE team for application-specific sizing analysis.

faqs.question: What is the self-discharge rate of the AW-2R7-J107UY?
faqs.answer: The AW-2R7-J107UY exhibits typical EDLC self-discharge characteristics. After charging to rated voltage and holding for 72 hours, the leakage current is specified at less than 0.3mA at 25°C. This corresponds to a voltage drop of approximately 10-15% over the first 24 hours, followed by slower discharge rates as voltage decreases. The self-discharge follows a logarithmic decay pattern characteristic of EDLC technology. At elevated temperatures, self-discharge increases significantly - approximately doubling for every 10°C increase. At 65°C, leakage current may reach 1-2mA. For applications requiring long-term energy retention (more than 72 hours), periodic topping charge or alternative energy storage technologies may be more appropriate. The high self-discharge rate is a fundamental characteristic of supercapacitors and must be considered in system design.
faqs.decisionGuide: If your application requires energy retention beyond 72 hours, consider hybrid capacitors with lower self-discharge or implement a maintenance charging circuit.

faqs.question: Can the AW-2R7-J107UY be used in series connections?
faqs.answer: Yes, the AW-2R7-J107UY can be connected in series to achieve higher operating voltages, but active voltage balancing is mandatory for reliable long-term operation. When cells are connected in series, voltage imbalance naturally occurs due to variations in capacitance and leakage current between individual cells. Without balancing, some cells may exceed their 2.7V rating while others remain undercharged, leading to accelerated degradation and potential failure. Aowei recommends using active balancing circuits that monitor and equalize cell voltages during both charge and discharge cycles. For a 16V module (6 cells in series), the AW-BAL-6S-2.7V balancing board is recommended. Passive balancing using resistors is not recommended as it increases self-discharge and provides limited balancing capability. All cells in a series string should be from the same production batch to minimize parameter variations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For series applications, purchase pre-assembled modules with integrated balancing or contact our FAE team for balancing circuit design guidance.

faqs.question: What safety considerations apply to the AW-2R7-J107UY?
faqs.answer: The AW-2R7-J107UY contains organic electrolyte and must be handled with appropriate safety precautions. Do not disassemble, puncture, or expose to temperatures exceeding 85°C as this may cause venting, fire, or explosion. In normal operation, the hermetic seal prevents electrolyte leakage. However, mechanical abuse or overvoltage conditions can cause cell venting - a safety feature that releases pressure to prevent rupture. The electrolyte is flammable; avoid exposure to ignition sources. First aid: if electrolyte contacts skin, wash immediately with water; if in eyes, flush with water for 15 minutes and seek medical attention. For disposal, supercapacitors are not classified as hazardous waste under most regulations, but local regulations should be consulted. Cells should be discharged to 0V before disposal. The product is UL recognized and meets applicable safety standards when used within specified parameters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the complete safety datasheet and application notes before designing the AW-2R7-J107UY into your system.

faqs.question: How does the AW-2R7-J107UY compare to battery solutions for backup power?
faqs.answer: The AW-2R7-J107UY offers distinct advantages and trade-offs compared to battery solutions for backup power applications. Advantages include: virtually unlimited cycle life (500,000+ vs 500-2000 for batteries), rapid charge capability (can charge to 90% in seconds vs hours for batteries), wide temperature operation (-40°C to +65°C vs limited range for batteries), maintenance-free operation, and no memory effect. Disadvantages include: lower energy density (0.1 Wh for this cell vs 1-3 Wh for similar-sized lithium batteries), higher self-discharge (10-15% per day vs 1-2% for lithium), and higher initial cost per watt-hour. For applications requiring frequent cycling, short backup times (seconds to minutes), wide temperature range, or maintenance-free operation, supercapacitors are superior. For long backup times (hours) and infrequent cycling, batteries remain more cost-effective. Hybrid solutions combining both technologies can leverage the advantages of each.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate your application's cycle frequency, backup duration, and temperature requirements to determine if supercapacitors or batteries are more suitable.

products.name: 220F 2.7V Cylindrical Supercapacitor
products.shortDescription: High-capacity 220F EDLC cell with ultra-low ESR for extended backup and high-power industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The AW-2R7-J227UY delivers 220 Farads of capacitance in a compact cylindrical form factor, providing double the energy storage of standard 100F cells while maintaining excellent power density.
With ESR as low as 12mΩ, this cell minimizes self-heating during high-current operation and delivers superior performance in pulse power and regenerative braking applications.
The enhanced electrode design and optimized electrolyte formulation provide exceptional cycle life exceeding 500,000 cycles with minimal degradation.
features: Ultra-low 12mΩ ESR for maximum power delivery

faeReview.author: Sarah Johnson
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The AW-2R7-J227UY is my top recommendation for industrial applications requiring both high energy and high power. The 12mΩ ESR is genuinely best-in-class - I've benchmarked it against equivalent cells from Maxwell, Nesscap, and Samwha, and the Aowei consistently shows 10-15% lower ESR. In a recent wind turbine pitch control application, we needed to store enough energy for three complete blade pitch cycles during grid loss. The 220F capacitance provided exactly the right energy buffer, and the low ESR meant we could discharge at 50A without excessive heating. One feature I particularly value is the consistent quality - we've processed over 10,000 units across multiple production lots and seen remarkably tight parameter distribution. For any application where both power density and reliability are critical, this cell delivers exceptional value.
faeReview.highlight: Best-in-class ESR performance with consistent quality across production lots

faqs.question: What is the maximum continuous current for the AW-2R7-J227UY?
faqs.answer: The AW-2R7-J227UY is rated for maximum continuous current of 12A at 25°C, limited by thermal considerations. At this current level, the power dissipation in the ESR (P = I² × R = 144 × 0.012 = 1.73W) must be managed through adequate heat sinking or thermal management. For short-duration pulses (less than 10 seconds), peak currents up to 50A are permissible, making this cell ideal for regenerative braking and pulse power applications. The current capability decreases at elevated temperatures - approximately 10A at 45°C and 7A at 65°C. For continuous high-current applications, thermal modeling is recommended to ensure cell temperature remains within specified limits. The low 12mΩ ESR minimizes self-heating compared to higher-resistance alternatives, enabling higher operational currents for a given temperature rise.
faqs.decisionGuide: For continuous currents above 10A, implement thermal management or parallel multiple cells to distribute current and heat load.

faqs.question: How does the AW-2R7-J227UY perform in regenerative braking applications?
faqs.answer: The AW-2R7-J227UY excels in regenerative braking applications due to its combination of high capacitance and low ESR. During braking events, the cell can accept high charge currents (up to 50A peak) with minimal voltage rise, maximizing energy recovery efficiency. The low ESR (12mΩ) means less energy is lost as heat during both charge and discharge cycles. For a typical EV regenerative braking scenario with 20kW braking power for 5 seconds, the cell voltage rises from 1.5V to approximately 2.4V, capturing about 85% of available braking energy. The 220F capacitance provides adequate energy storage for multiple braking cycles without requiring immediate discharge. The cell's 500,000+ cycle life easily accommodates the frequent charge/discharge cycles typical of urban driving profiles. For system design, six cells in series create a 16V module suitable for 12V automotive systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: For EV regenerative braking systems, use 6 cells in series (16V module) with active balancing. Contact our FAE team for system sizing calculations.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of the AW-2R7-J227UY?
faqs.answer: The AW-2R7-J227UY has a thermal resistance (Rth) from case to ambient of approximately 15°C/W in free air conditions. This means that for every watt of power dissipated in the ESR, the cell case temperature rises 15°C above ambient. For example, at 10A continuous current, power dissipation is 1.2W (10² × 0.012), resulting in an 18°C temperature rise. The thermal resistance can be significantly reduced through mounting techniques - securing the cell to a metal chassis or PCB ground plane can reduce Rth to 8-10°C/W. Forced air cooling further improves thermal performance. The cell's maximum operating temperature of 65°C limits continuous operation in high ambient temperatures without additional cooling. For thermal modeling, use the following parameters: Rth_case-ambient = 15°C/W, thermal time constant ≈ 300 seconds, maximum internal temperature = 70°C.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-current applications, ensure adequate thermal management through PCB design or chassis mounting. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling support.

faqs.question: Can the AW-2R7-J227UY be used in parallel configurations?
faqs.answer: Yes, parallel connection of AW-2R7-J227UY cells is a common practice to increase total capacitance and current capability. When connecting cells in parallel, total capacitance is the sum of individual capacitances (C_total = n × C_cell), while ESR decreases proportionally (ESR_total = ESR_cell / n). For example, two 220F cells in parallel provide 440F capacitance with 6mΩ effective ESR. Parallel configurations improve current sharing and reduce self-heating. However, important considerations apply: cells should be from the same production batch to minimize voltage imbalance, initial charging should be performed with cells at similar voltages to avoid high equalization currents, and wiring resistance between cells must be minimized to ensure current sharing. Parallel configurations do not require balancing circuits since cells naturally equalize voltage. This topology is ideal for high-current applications where single-cell ESR would cause excessive heating.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring capacitance above 220F or current above 12A continuous, parallel multiple AW-2R7-J227UY cells.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the AW-2R7-J227UY in typical applications?
faqs.answer: The expected lifetime of the AW-2R7-J227UY depends strongly on operating conditions, particularly voltage and temperature. Under nominal conditions (2.7V, 25°C), the cell is rated for 500,000 cycles with less than 20% capacitance degradation. Calendar life is typically 10+ years at 25°C and rated voltage. Using the Arrhenius equation, lifetime approximately doubles for every 10°C decrease in temperature. For example, at 45°C, calendar life reduces to approximately 2.5 years, while at 65°C it may be less than 1 year. Voltage derating significantly extends lifetime - operating at 2.43V (90% of rated) can extend calendar life by 2-3x. For applications requiring maximum lifetime, Aowei recommends operating at 80% of rated voltage and below 45°C. The cell's built-in voltage derating characteristics and robust construction make it suitable for 10+ year applications when properly designed.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring 10+ year lifetime, implement voltage derating to 2.4V and ensure operating temperature remains below 45°C.

faqs.question: What testing has the AW-2R7-J227UY undergone for quality assurance?
faqs.answer: The AW-2R7-J227UY undergoes comprehensive testing to ensure quality and reliability. 100% production testing includes capacitance measurement (within ±20% tolerance), ESR testing (must be ≤12mΩ), leakage current measurement (≤0.5mA at 72 hours), and visual inspection. Additional qualification testing per AEC-Q200 includes: high temperature storage (1000 hours at 85°C), temperature cycling (-40°C to +85°C, 1000 cycles), mechanical shock (50G, 11ms), vibration (5-2000Hz, 20G), and humidity resistance (85°C/85%RH, 1000 hours). Life testing demonstrates 500,000+ cycles with less than 20% degradation. Safety testing includes overvoltage, reverse voltage, short circuit, and nail penetration tests. All testing is performed in ISO 17025 accredited laboratories. Certificate of conformance and detailed test reports are available upon request for high-reliability applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request PPAP documentation and qualification test reports for automotive or high-reliability applications requiring detailed quality documentation.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of prismatic supercapacitors over cylindrical cells?
faqs.answer: Prismatic supercapacitors offer several key advantages for specific applications. The rectangular form factor provides superior volumetric efficiency, allowing more energy storage in a given volume compared to cylindrical cells. The thin profile (as low as 2.5mm) enables integration into space-constrained designs such as smartphones, tablets, and wearable devices. The aluminum laminate packaging is significantly lighter than metal can construction, reducing overall system weight. Thermal performance is enhanced due to the larger surface area to volume ratio, enabling better heat dissipation. Mounting flexibility is improved with flat surfaces suitable for adhesive bonding or clamping. However, prismatic cells typically have lower capacitance values per cell and may require more complex handling during assembly compared to cylindrical cells with leads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose prismatic cells for applications with strict height constraints or where maximum volumetric efficiency is required. Contact our FAE team for mechanical integration guidance.

faqs.question: How do I mount prismatic supercapacitors in my design?
faqs.answer: Prismatic supercapacitors can be mounted using several methods depending on your application requirements. Double-sided adhesive tape is the most common mounting method, providing secure attachment while accommodating thermal expansion. The mounting surface should be clean, flat, and thermally conductive when possible. Mechanical clamping using a compression plate distributes pressure evenly across the cell surface and allows for easy replacement. PCB mounting using solder tabs or wire leads is suitable for smaller cells in low-vibration environments. For high-vibration applications (automotive, industrial), additional mechanical retention such as brackets or enclosures is recommended. Avoid puncturing, folding, or applying excessive force to the cell edges. Maintain minimum bend radius requirements for any attached leads. The mounting orientation (flat vs edge) affects thermal performance and should be considered in the design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the mechanical application notes for detailed mounting guidelines and recommended adhesives for your operating temperature range.

faqs.question: What is the typical thickness range for Aowei prismatic supercapacitors?
faqs.answer: Aowei prismatic supercapacitors are available in thicknesses ranging from 2.5mm to 12mm depending on capacitance value and series. The ultra-thin AW-PZ series offers 2.5mm to 4.5mm profiles for extremely space-constrained applications such as smart cards and wearables. Standard AW-PS series cells range from 5mm to 8mm, suitable for most portable electronics and IoT devices. High-capacity AW-PH series cells for industrial applications range from 8mm to 12mm. The thin profile is achieved through optimized electrode stacking and advanced packaging technology. When selecting a cell thickness, consider not only the nominal cell thickness but also any additional space required for mounting hardware, thermal interface materials, or protective enclosures. The flexible packaging allows slight compression during mounting but should not be over-constrained.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify your maximum allowable thickness when requesting recommendations. We can provide detailed mechanical drawings including tolerances.

faqs.question: Are prismatic supercapacitors suitable for wearable device applications?
faqs.answer: Prismatic supercapacitors are excellent choices for wearable device applications due to their unique characteristics. The thin, flexible form factor conforms better to curved surfaces and tight spaces typical of wearables. The lightweight construction (up to 50% lighter than equivalent cylindrical cells) minimizes device weight for user comfort. High power density enables rapid charging - most wearable applications can fully charge in 1-3 minutes. The wide operating temperature range (-40°C to +65°C) accommodates body-worn devices in various environments. Exceptional cycle life (500,000+ cycles) eliminates battery replacement concerns for device lifetime. Low self-discharge compared to batteries reduces standby power consumption. However, the lower energy density compared to lithium batteries means supercapacitors are best suited for applications with frequent charging opportunities or where burst power is required rather than long runtime.
faqs.decisionGuide: For wearable applications, consider the AW-PZ series with thickness under 4mm. Contact our FAE team for integration examples from similar wearable products.

faqs.question: What are the packaging and handling requirements for prismatic supercapacitors?
faqs.answer: Prismatic supercapacitors require careful handling due to their flexible aluminum laminate packaging. Cells should be stored in original packaging until ready for use to prevent physical damage or contamination. Avoid sharp objects, bending, or puncturing the cell package. The sealing edges are particularly sensitive to mechanical stress. Handle cells with clean, dry hands or gloves to prevent contamination. Do not stack heavy objects on cells during storage or assembly. Maximum storage temperature is 35°C; avoid direct sunlight or heat sources. Humidity should be maintained below 60% RH. Cells are shipped at approximately 50% state of charge for safety. Shelf life in unopened packaging is 2 years at room temperature. During assembly, avoid applying pressure to the cell face beyond 0.5 MPa. Use appropriate ESD protection during handling and assembly.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the handling and storage guidelines document before receiving your first shipment of prismatic cells.

The aluminum laminate packaging provides excellent volumetric efficiency while maintaining the same 500,000+ cycle life and wide temperature range of larger supercapacitors.

I've used this cell in several smartwatch projects where it provides burst power for haptic feedback and wireless transmission without the bulk of a battery. The 150mΩ ESR is reasonable for the size - we've achieved 2A pulse currents without excessive voltage droop. For any ultra-compact application, this cell delivers remarkable performance.

faqs.question: What is the maximum pulse current for the AW-PZ-2R7-505?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-505 is rated for maximum pulse current of 2A for durations up to 1 second. This rating is limited by the cell's 150mΩ ESR and thermal constraints of the thin packaging. At 2A, the voltage drop across ESR is approximately 0.3V (2A × 0.15Ω), which should be considered in system design. For longer pulses, the current should be derated to prevent excessive heating. Continuous current is limited to 0.3A to maintain safe operating temperatures. The pulse capability makes this cell ideal for wireless transmission bursts, LED flash, and haptic feedback applications typical in wearables. For higher current requirements, consider parallel connection of multiple cells or selecting a higher capacitance model with lower ESR.
faqs.decisionGuide: For pulse currents above 2A, parallel multiple cells or select the 10F model with lower ESR.

faqs.question: How do I charge the AW-PZ-2R7-505 safely?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-505 should be charged using a current-limited power source or dedicated supercapacitor charger IC. Maximum recommended charge current is 0.5A (0.1C rate) for standard charging. Fast charging up to 2A is possible but will reduce cycle life slightly. The cell can be charged from any voltage source capable of providing 2.7V with current limiting. Simple charging can be implemented using a resistor in series with a voltage source, though this is inefficient. Better solutions use active current limiting circuits or dedicated charger ICs that provide constant current followed by constant voltage (CC-CV) charging profile. Charge time from 0V to 90% is approximately 5 × R × C when using resistor limiting, or about 120 seconds at 0.5A constant current. Always include overvoltage protection to prevent exceeding 2.7V.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use a dedicated supercapacitor charger IC for optimal performance and safety. Contact our FAE team for recommended charger ICs and reference circuits.

faqs.question: What is the self-discharge characteristic of the AW-PZ-2R7-505?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-505 exhibits typical EDLC self-discharge behavior with leakage current less than 0.02mA at 25°C after 72 hours. This corresponds to a voltage drop of approximately 15-20% in the first 24 hours after charging to 2.7V, followed by slower discharge. The self-discharge rate follows the logarithmic decay pattern common to all EDLC supercapacitors. At elevated temperatures, self-discharge increases significantly - approximately doubling for every 10°C rise. At 65°C, leakage current may reach 0.08mA. For applications requiring energy retention beyond 48 hours, periodic maintenance charging or alternative energy storage should be considered. The self-discharge characteristic is a fundamental property of the EDLC technology and must be accounted for in system power budgets.
faqs.decisionGuide: If your application requires energy retention beyond 48 hours, consider implementing a maintenance charging circuit or contact us for alternative solutions.

faqs.question: Can the AW-PZ-2R7-505 be used in series for higher voltages?
faqs.answer: Yes, the AW-PZ-2R7-505 can be connected in series for higher voltage applications, but active voltage balancing is essential for reliable operation. When series-connected, voltage imbalance occurs due to cell-to-cell variations in capacitance and leakage current. Without balancing, individual cells may exceed their 2.7V rating, causing accelerated degradation. For two cells in series (5.4V total), simple passive balancing using 1kΩ resistors may be sufficient for low-duty applications. For three or more cells, active balancing using dedicated ICs is strongly recommended. The balancing circuit should equalize both charge and discharge cycles. Cells should be from the same production batch to minimize parameter variation. The series configuration reduces total capacitance (C_total = C_cell / n) while multiplying voltage (V_total = V_cell × n).
faqs.decisionGuide: For series applications of 3+ cells, use an active balancing IC. Contact our FAE team for balancing circuit recommendations.

faqs.question: What are the best practices for soldering connections to the AW-PZ-2R7-505?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-505 features nickel-plated copper tabs for electrical connection. Soldering should be performed using a temperature-controlled iron set to 350°C maximum with soldering time limited to 3 seconds per tab. Use rosin-core solder (SN63/PB37 or lead-free SAC305) with RMA flux. Pre-tinning the tabs before final assembly is recommended. Avoid applying excessive heat or mechanical stress to the cell body during soldering. The aluminum laminate package is sensitive to heat - keep the iron tip away from the cell body. For high-volume production, resistance welding or laser welding of tabs is preferred over soldering. After soldering, allow the cell to cool to room temperature before applying power. Do not bend the tabs closer than 3mm to the cell body to prevent seal damage. Use appropriate ESD protection during handling and assembly.
faqs.decisionGuide: For prototype quantities, hand soldering with temperature control is acceptable. For production volumes, consider welded connections or contact us for assembly recommendations.

faqs.question: How does the AW-PZ-2R7-505 compare to small lithium batteries for wearable applications?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-505 offers distinct trade-offs compared to small lithium batteries for wearable applications. Advantages include: virtually unlimited cycle life (500,000+ vs 300-500 for small Li-ion), rapid charging (minutes vs hours), wide temperature operation (-40°C to +65°C), maintenance-free operation, and no safety concerns about fire or explosion. The thin form factor (2.5mm) is competitive with or thinner than equivalent capacity lithium polymer cells. Disadvantages include: lower energy density (0.005 Wh vs 0.1-0.2 Wh for similar-sized Li-ion), higher self-discharge (15-20% per day vs 1-2%), and voltage droop during discharge. For wearables with frequent charging opportunities (daily or more) and burst power requirements, supercapacitors excel. For devices requiring multi-day runtime between charges, batteries remain more suitable. Many wearable designs now use hybrid approaches combining both technologies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate your device's charging frequency and runtime requirements. For daily-charged devices with burst power needs, the AW-PZ-2R7-505 is an excellent choice.

faqs.question: What is the maximum continuous current for the AW-PZ-2R7-108?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-108 is rated for maximum continuous current of 0.6A at 25°C, limited by thermal considerations of the thin package. At this current, power dissipation in the ESR is approximately 29mW (0.6² × 0.08), resulting in minimal temperature rise. For pulse applications, currents up to 3A are permissible for durations up to 1 second. The higher current capability compared to the 5F model is due to the lower 80mΩ ESR. For applications requiring sustained currents above 0.6A, parallel connection of multiple cells or selecting a higher-capacitance model is recommended. Current capability decreases at elevated temperatures - approximately 0.5A at 45°C and 0.35A at 65°C. Always verify actual operating temperature in your application.
faqs.decisionGuide: For continuous currents above 0.6A, parallel multiple cells or select the 30F model with lower ESR.

faqs.question: How much backup time can the AW-PZ-2R7-108 provide?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-108 stores approximately 0.01 Wh of energy (E = 0.5 × 10F × 2.7V² = 36.45 Joules). Backup time depends on load current and allowable voltage drop. For a constant 50mA load from 2.7V to 1.35V (50% voltage drop), backup time is approximately 10F × 1.35V / 0.05A = 270 seconds or 4.5 minutes. At 100mA load, backup time reduces to approximately 2.25 minutes. For burst applications typical in IoT devices (e.g., 500mA for 1 second every minute), the cell can support thousands of transmission cycles. The actual usable energy should be calculated using E_usable = 0.5 × C × (V_max² - V_min²) where V_min is your system's minimum operating voltage. For precise calculations, contact our FAE team with your specific load profile.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate your specific backup requirements or contact our FAE team for application-specific sizing analysis.

faqs.question: What are the recommended charging parameters for the AW-PZ-2R7-108?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-108 should be charged using a current-limited source with the following parameters: Maximum charge current: 1A (0.1C rate) for standard charging, up to 3A for fast charging with reduced cycle life. Charge voltage: 2.7V maximum, with 2.5V recommended for extended cycle life. Charge profile: Constant current (CC) until reaching charge voltage, then constant voltage (CV) until current tapers to 0.01C. Charge time from fully discharged: approximately 15 seconds at 3A fast charge, or 45 seconds at 1A standard charge to reach 90% capacity. Simple resistor-limited charging can be used for low-cost applications, though this is less efficient. Always include overvoltage protection (2.85V absolute maximum) to prevent cell damage. The cell can accept charge at temperatures from -40°C to +65°C, though charging at elevated temperatures reduces cycle life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use a dedicated supercapacitor charger IC for optimal performance. Contact our FAE team for recommended charger circuits and ICs.

faqs.question: Can the AW-PZ-2R7-108 be used in outdoor applications?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-108 is suitable for outdoor applications within its specified operating temperature range of -40°C to +65°C. The aluminum laminate packaging is sealed against moisture and dust when properly handled. For outdoor installations, additional environmental protection is recommended: enclosure with IP65 rating or better, UV protection if exposed to direct sunlight, and protection from physical damage. The wide temperature range accommodates most outdoor environments, though performance varies with temperature. At low temperatures (-40°C), ESR increases to approximately 300mΩ, limiting high-current capability. At high temperatures (+65°C), leakage current increases and cycle life is reduced. For extreme outdoor applications, consider thermal management or selecting cells specifically rated for extended temperature ranges. The cell is not rated for submersion or high-humidity condensation environments without additional protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: For outdoor applications, ensure adequate enclosure protection. Contact our FAE team for environmental sealing recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the shelf life of the AW-PZ-2R7-108?
faqs.answer: The AW-PZ-2R7-108 has a shelf life of 2 years when stored in original unopened packaging at temperatures between -20°C and +35°C and relative humidity below 60%. After 2 years, the cell may require additional charging cycles to achieve full performance. Storage at elevated temperatures accelerates aging and should be avoided. The cell is shipped at approximately 50% state of charge for safety and longevity. During storage, some voltage drop due to self-discharge is normal - the cell may drop to 0.5V or lower over extended storage periods. This does not damage the cell; normal capacity is restored after charging. For long-term storage beyond 2 years, cells should be charged to 2.0-2.5V and stored in a cool, dry environment. Always check cell voltage before use after extended storage and charge if below 0.5V.
faqs.decisionGuide: Rotate inventory to use cells within 2 years of receipt. Contact us for long-term storage recommendations if needed.

faqs.question: How do I determine if the AW-PZ-2R7-108 has been damaged during handling?
faqs.answer: Visual inspection can identify most handling damage to the AW-PZ-2R7-108. Check for: punctures or tears in the aluminum laminate packaging, swelling or bulging of the cell body, electrolyte leakage (oily residue), bent or damaged tabs, and discoloration of the packaging. Electrical testing should measure: capacitance within ±20% of rated 10F, ESR less than 80mΩ, and leakage current less than 0.03mA at 72 hours. Significant deviation from these parameters may indicate damage. A damaged cell should not be used - dispose of according to local regulations. If electrolyte contacts skin, wash immediately with water. The cell contains organic electrolyte that can be irritating but is not highly toxic. Damaged cells may vent gas or heat if charged - never charge a visibly damaged cell.
faqs.decisionGuide: Perform incoming inspection on all cells. Reject any cells showing visible damage or out-of-spec electrical parameters.

The pouch cell construction offers superior thermal performance and reduced weight compared to cylindrical alternatives, while maintaining the same exceptional cycle life and power density characteristics. As an authorized Aowei distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive selection guidance, technical support, and competitive pricing for the complete Prismatic Supercapacitors product line. categories.description: Aowei's module systems integrate multiple supercapacitor cells with advanced active balancing, thermal management, and monitoring capabilities to create complete energy storage solutions. Available in standard voltages from 16V to 160V, these modules eliminate the complexity of designing custom series-parallel configurations. Each module features cell voltage balancing circuits that maintain voltage equalization during charge and discharge cycles, extending system life and ensuring safe operation. Integrated temperature monitoring, overvoltage protection, and robust mechanical packaging make these modules ready for immediate integration into electric vehicles, industrial systems, and grid-scale energy storage applications.

selectionGuide.title: Module System Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the optimal module voltage and capacitance for your application

faqs.question: What voltage options are available for Aowei module systems?
faqs.answer: Aowei module systems are available in standard voltages ranging from 16V to 160V to match common system requirements. Standard offerings include: 16V modules (6 cells in series) for 12V automotive systems and low-voltage industrial applications; 48V modules (18 cells) for telecom backup, mild hybrid vehicles, and industrial automation; 96V modules (36 cells) for electric vehicle subsystems and larger industrial equipment; and 160V modules (60 cells) for heavy-duty EV applications and grid-scale energy storage. Custom voltages can be configured for volume applications. Each module includes the appropriate number of 2.7V cells in series with active balancing circuits. The module voltage should be selected based on your system voltage with appropriate margin for charge regulation. Higher voltage modules reduce current requirements for a given power level, reducing I²R losses in wiring.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select a module voltage that matches or slightly exceeds your system voltage. Contact our FAE team for voltage selection guidance and custom configurations.

faqs.question: How does active balancing work in Aowei modules?
faqs.answer: Aowei modules employ active balancing circuits that continuously monitor and equalize individual cell voltages during both charge and discharge cycles. The balancing system uses switched capacitor or inductive transfer topologies to move charge from higher-voltage cells to lower-voltage cells. This prevents any single cell from exceeding its 2.7V rating while ensuring all cells reach full charge. Balancing current typically ranges from 50mA to 200mA depending on module size, sufficient to handle typical cell mismatch. The balancing circuit operates automatically without external control - simply apply charge voltage to the module terminals. LED indicators or communication interfaces (CAN, Modbus on select models) provide status indication. Active balancing significantly extends module life compared to passive resistor balancing by eliminating energy waste and providing faster, more effective equalization. The balancing system is powered by the module itself and requires no external power supply.
faqs.decisionGuide: All Aowei modules include active balancing as standard. No additional balancing components are required for proper operation.

faqs.question: What monitoring and protection features are included in Aowei modules?
faqs.answer: Aowei modules include comprehensive monitoring and protection features to ensure safe, reliable operation. Voltage monitoring tracks individual cell voltages and overall module voltage with overvoltage protection that disconnects the module if any cell exceeds 2.8V. Temperature monitoring uses multiple sensors to detect abnormal heating with thermal shutdown at 70°C. Current monitoring protects against overcurrent conditions with automatic reset. Communication interfaces (available on select models) provide real-time data via CAN bus or Modbus RTU including: individual cell voltages, module voltage, temperatures, state of charge estimation, and fault status. Mechanical protection includes rugged aluminum enclosures, vibration isolation, and IP54 or IP65 environmental sealing options. Safety features include pressure relief vents, internal fusing, and isolation monitoring for high-voltage modules. These integrated features reduce system design complexity and ensure safe operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select modules with CAN/Modbus communication for systems requiring detailed monitoring. Basic modules include essential protection without communication.

faqs.question: How do I select the right module capacitance for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the appropriate module capacitance requires calculating your energy and power requirements. First, determine the energy needed in watt-hours based on your load power and required backup time. Remember that usable energy is E = 1/2 × C × (V_max² - V_min²) where V_min is your system's minimum operating voltage. For example, a 16V module (6 cells) has V_max = 16.2V and if your system operates down to 12V, usable voltage is 4.2V. Module capacitance is related to cell capacitance by C_module = C_cell / n where n is the number of series cells. A 16V module using 100F cells has C_module = 100F/6 = 16.7F. Calculate required capacitance: C = 2 × E / (V_max² - V_min²). For 10 Wh usable energy in a 16V module: C = 2 × 36000 / (16.2² - 12²) = 72000 / 118 = 610F required. This would require a module with 610F × 6 = 3660F cell capacitance, achievable with parallel cell configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your energy and power requirements for detailed module sizing calculations and recommendations.

faqs.question: What are the thermal management requirements for module systems?
faqs.answer: Aowei modules include integrated thermal management but may require additional cooling depending on application duty cycle. The aluminum enclosure acts as a heat sink, dissipating heat generated in cell ESR during charge/discharge cycles. Modules are rated for natural convection cooling at specified continuous current ratings. For high-duty applications, forced air cooling using fans can increase continuous current capability by 30-50%. Mounting the module to a metal chassis or heat sink further improves thermal performance. Ambient temperature must be maintained within -20°C to +45°C for rated performance; extended temperature ranges are possible with derating. High-voltage modules (96V and above) include internal temperature monitoring with automatic current limiting at elevated temperatures. Thermal modeling is recommended for applications with continuous currents above 50% of rated maximum. Contact our FAE team for thermal analysis of your specific application.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-power applications, ensure adequate airflow or chassis mounting. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling support.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the AW-MOD-16V-33F module?
faqs.answer: The AW-MOD-16V-33F module is designed for a service life of 10+ years in typical automotive and industrial applications. The underlying cells are rated for 500,000 cycles, and the active balancing system ensures even wear across all cells. At 25°C ambient temperature and typical duty cycles (10-20% daily depth of discharge), calendar life exceeds 10 years. The module's life is primarily determined by the cells, with the balancing and protection electronics designed for 15+ year reliability. High-temperature operation accelerates aging according to the Arrhenius relationship - operating at 45°C reduces calendar life to approximately 5 years, while 65°C operation may yield 2-3 years. Voltage derating (operating at 14V instead of 16V) can significantly extend lifetime. The module includes end-of-life prediction algorithms that monitor capacitance degradation and provide early warning when replacement is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum module lifetime, operate below 45°C and consider voltage derating to 14V nominal.

faqs.question: How do I install and connect the AW-MOD-16V-33F module?
faqs.answer: Installation of the AW-MOD-16V-33F requires attention to mechanical mounting, electrical connections, and thermal management. Mount the module using the integrated flanges with M6 hardware, applying vibration isolation washers in automotive applications. Torque mounting bolts to 6-8 Nm. Ensure adequate airflow around the module; if mounted in an enclosed space, provide ventilation or forced air cooling for high-duty applications. Electrical connections use M8 brass terminals - use 4AWG or larger cable with ring terminals for main power connections. Torque terminal nuts to 8-10 Nm. Connect positive to (+) terminal, negative to (-) terminal. The module has no polarity protection - reverse connection will damage the unit. No external balancing or protection circuitry is required - these functions are integrated. For systems with multiple modules, connect in parallel for increased capacitance, not in series. Always install appropriate fusing external to the module.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the installation manual for detailed mounting and wiring instructions. Contact our FAE team for application-specific installation guidance.

faqs.question: What charging system is required for the AW-MOD-16V-33F?
faqs.answer: The AW-MOD-16V-33F can be charged using any current-limited DC power source capable of providing up to 17V. Recommended charging parameters: Maximum charge voltage: 16.2V (6 × 2.7V), Charge current: Up to 25A continuous, 50A peak. The module accepts charge at any rate up to maximum current - no complex charging algorithm is required. Simple voltage-limited charging works well; current naturally tapers as the module approaches full charge. For automotive applications, the module can connect directly to the 14.5V vehicle charging system with appropriate current limiting. For energy harvesting applications, a DC-DC converter with voltage limiting is recommended. The integrated balancing circuit handles charge distribution automatically. Charge time from empty to 90% is approximately (C × V) / I = (33F × 16V) / 25A = 21 seconds at maximum current. Always include overvoltage protection set to 17V maximum to prevent damage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard automotive charging systems work well. For custom applications, use a current-limited supply set to 16.2V maximum.

faqs.question: Can multiple AW-MOD-16V-33F modules be connected in parallel?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple AW-MOD-16V-33F modules can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and current capability. Parallel connection sums capacitance (two modules provide 66F) while reducing effective ESR (two modules provide 60mΩ). This configuration is ideal for applications requiring more energy storage or higher continuous current than a single module can provide. When connecting modules in parallel: ensure all modules are at similar voltage before connection to prevent high equalization currents, use equal length cables from each module to the common connection point for current sharing, and fuse each module individually for protection. No special balancing is required between parallel modules as they self-equalize. Up to 4 modules can typically be paralleled without additional considerations; larger arrays may require contactors for staged connection during startup. Parallel modules operate independently - if one module fails, the others continue operating.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring more than 25A continuous or 4.3Wh energy, parallel multiple modules.

faqs.question: What maintenance is required for the AW-MOD-16V-33F module?
faqs.answer: The AW-MOD-16V-33F module is designed for maintenance-free operation throughout its service life. Unlike batteries, supercapacitor modules require no periodic watering, equalization charging, or capacity testing. Recommended maintenance consists of: Annual visual inspection for physical damage, corrosion, or loose connections; verification of mounting hardware torque; cleaning of enclosure and terminals if operating in dusty environments; and monitoring of module performance indicators if equipped with communication interface. The LED status indicators provide basic health status - green indicates normal operation, red indicates a fault condition requiring investigation. If the module includes CAN/Modbus communication, periodic monitoring of cell voltages can detect developing issues before they affect performance. No preventive replacement of components is required. At end of life (typically 10+ years or when capacitance drops below 80% of rated), the entire module should be replaced. The module is not user-serviceable - do not attempt to disassemble.
faqs.decisionGuide: Include annual visual inspection in your system maintenance schedule. No other maintenance is required.

faqs.question: What safety certifications does the AW-MOD-16V-33F module carry?
faqs.answer: The AW-MOD-16V-33F module carries multiple safety certifications for global market acceptance. The module is UL 1973 recognized for use in stationary and vehicle auxiliary power applications. It meets UN 38.3 transportation requirements for lithium batteries (applicable to supercapacitor modules for shipping classification). The module complies with RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU for restriction of hazardous substances. faqs.question: How do I communicate with the AW-MOD-48V-11F via CAN bus?
faqs.answer: The AW-MOD-48V-11F implements CAN 2.0B communication at 250 kbps or 500 kbps (configurable). The module transmits status messages at 100ms intervals containing: module voltage (0.1V resolution), individual cell voltages (1mV resolution), module current (0.1A resolution), temperatures from 4 sensors (0.1°C resolution), state of charge estimate (1% resolution), and fault status flags. The module accepts command messages for: sleep/wake control, current limit configuration, and balancing enable/disable. A DBC file is provided for easy integration with standard CAN tools and vehicle networks. The CAN interface is isolated to 500V from the power terminals. Termination resistor (120Ω) should be provided externally. For systems with multiple modules, each module can be assigned a unique node ID via DIP switches or software configuration. Contact our FAE team for the complete communication protocol specification and example code.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request the DBC file and protocol specification from our support team for integration into your CAN network.

faqs.question: What protection features are built into the AW-MOD-48V-11F?
faqs.answer: The AW-MOD-48V-11F includes comprehensive protection features to ensure safe operation and prevent damage. Overvoltage protection monitors individual cell voltages and module voltage, disconnecting the module via internal contactors if any cell exceeds 2.85V or module voltage exceeds 51V. Undervoltage protection disconnects the load if module voltage drops below 36V to prevent over-discharge. Overcurrent protection limits current to 25A continuous with automatic recovery; sustained overcurrent above 100A triggers contactor opening. Overtemperature protection reduces current limit at 55°C and disconnects at 70°C. Short circuit protection triggers immediate contactor opening with manual reset required. The module also includes reverse polarity protection on the communication interface and isolation monitoring for high-voltage safety. All protection events are logged in non-volatile memory with timestamps for diagnostic purposes. Fault status is indicated via CAN messages and LED indicators.
faqs.decisionGuide: The module is self-protecting but external fusing is still recommended for system-level protection.

faqs.question: How do I size a 48V system using the AW-MOD-48V-11F?
faqs.answer: Sizing a 48V system with the AW-MOD-48V-11F requires calculating energy requirements and determining the number of parallel modules needed. The module stores approximately 4.0 Wh of usable energy (E = 0.5 × 11F × (48.6V² - 40V²) = 4.0 Wh assuming discharge from 48.6V to 40V). For example, a system requiring 2kW for 10 seconds needs 20 kJ = 5.6 Wh, requiring two modules in parallel (8.0 Wh total, providing margin). For power requirements: the module can deliver 20A continuous (960W) and 80A peak (3.8kW for 10s). If your application requires 4kW continuous, you need 5 modules in parallel (100A total capability). When paralleling modules, ensure equal cable lengths for current sharing. The CAN communication allows monitoring of all modules from a single interface. For systems with more than 4 modules, staged startup using contactors is recommended to limit inrush current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your power and energy requirements for detailed system sizing and configuration recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the thermal performance of the AW-MOD-48V-11F?
faqs.answer: The AW-MOD-48V-11F thermal performance depends on operating conditions and cooling methods. The aluminum enclosure provides natural convection cooling rated for 20A continuous current at 25°C ambient. Internal temperature rise at 20A is approximately 15°C above ambient due to I²R heating (400W × 0.2Ω = 0.8W per cell × 18 cells = 14.4W total heat). Four internal temperature sensors monitor cell temperatures and enable active thermal management. At 45°C ambient, continuous current should be derated to 15A to maintain safe internal temperatures. Forced air cooling (200 LFM) increases continuous current capability to 30A. Mounting the module to a metal chassis or heat sink can improve thermal performance by 20-30%. The module includes thermal derating - current limit is automatically reduced if internal temperatures exceed 55°C. For high-duty applications, thermal modeling is recommended to ensure adequate cooling.
faqs.decisionGuide: For continuous operation above 20A or ambient temperatures above 45°C, implement forced air cooling or chassis mounting.

faqs.question: Can the AW-MOD-48V-11F be used in marine applications?
faqs.answer: The AW-MOD-48V-11F is well-suited for marine applications with appropriate installation considerations. The IP65 enclosure provides protection against water jets and dust, suitable for protected marine installations. For exposed locations or potential submersion, additional enclosure protection is required. The wide operating temperature range accommodates marine environments from arctic to tropical conditions. The module's resistance to shock and vibration meets marine requirements. Salt spray protection is provided by the anodized aluminum enclosure and sealed connectors. Common marine applications include: electric propulsion auxiliary power, winch and windlass energy storage, navigation system backup, and hybrid vessel energy recovery. The CAN communication interfaces well with marine monitoring systems. For installation, ensure adequate ventilation in engine compartments where temperatures may exceed 45°C. Use marine-grade cable and connectors for all electrical connections.
faqs.decisionGuide: For marine installations, specify the optional conformal coating and stainless steel hardware. Contact our FAE team for marine certification requirements.

faqs.question: What is the warranty for the AW-MOD-48V-11F module?
faqs.answer: The AW-MOD-48V-11F carries a standard 3-year warranty against defects in materials and workmanship under normal use and service. categories.name: Hybrid Capacitors
categories.description: Aowei's hybrid capacitors, also known as lithium-ion capacitors (LIC), combine the high energy density of lithium-ion batteries with the high power density and long cycle life of EDLC supercapacitors. Using a pre-doped lithium-ion anode with an activated carbon cathode, these cells achieve 3-5 times the energy density of standard EDLC while maintaining excellent power characteristics. With nominal voltage of 3.8V and energy density up to 30 Wh/kg, hybrid capacitors are ideal for applications requiring both significant energy storage and rapid charge/discharge capability. Applications include wearable devices, wireless sensors, portable medical equipment, and backup systems requiring extended hold-up time.

selectionGuide.title: Hybrid Capacitor Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting between hybrid capacitors and EDLC based on energy and power requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between hybrid capacitors and EDLC supercapacitors?
faqs.answer: Hybrid capacitors (LIC) and EDLC supercapacitors differ in their electrode construction and performance characteristics. EDLC uses activated carbon for both anode and cathode, storing energy through electrostatic charge separation. Hybrid capacitors use a pre-doped lithium-ion anode with activated carbon cathode, combining battery-like energy storage with capacitor-like power delivery. Key differences: Energy density - Hybrid capacitors achieve 20-30 Wh/kg vs 5-10 Wh/kg for EDLC, providing 3-5x longer backup time. Voltage - Hybrid capacitors operate at 3.8V vs 2.7V for EDLC, reducing the number of cells needed for a given system voltage. Cycle life - EDLC offers 500,000+ cycles vs 50,000-100,000 for hybrid capacitors. Self-discharge - Hybrid capacitors have lower self-discharge (5-10% per day vs 15-20% for EDLC). Cost - Hybrid capacitors are typically 2-3x more expensive per farad than EDLC. The choice depends on whether your application prioritizes energy density (choose hybrid) or cycle life/cost (choose EDLC).
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose hybrid capacitors for applications requiring backup times over 60 seconds. Choose EDLC for high-cycle applications with frequent charge/discharge.

faqs.question: What are the optimal applications for hybrid capacitors?
faqs.answer: Hybrid capacitors excel in applications requiring both significant energy storage and good power density, particularly where battery replacement is impractical. Optimal applications include: Wearable devices where the thin profile and light weight complement compact designs, and daily charging is acceptable. Wireless sensors and IoT devices requiring months of standby with periodic data transmission bursts. Portable medical devices needing reliable backup power with long shelf life. Smart meters and utility devices requiring 10+ year operational life with minimal maintenance. Asset tracking devices operating in wide temperature ranges where batteries perform poorly. Emergency lighting requiring instant-on capability with extended runtime. Solar-powered devices needing energy storage for nighttime operation. Applications requiring rapid charge (minutes) combined with hours of runtime. Hybrid capacitors are less suitable for applications requiring 500,000+ cycles or continuous high-power operation where EDLC would be more appropriate.
faqs.decisionGuide: If your application needs 1-60 minutes of runtime with periodic charging, hybrid capacitors are likely the optimal choice.

faqs.question: How do I charge hybrid capacitors safely?
faqs.answer: Hybrid capacitors require careful charging to prevent lithium plating and ensure long cycle life. Key charging requirements: Charge voltage must not exceed 3.8V - overvoltage causes lithium metal plating on the anode, permanently damaging the cell. Charge current should be limited to 1C (1A per Farad) maximum; 0.5C is recommended for extended cycle life. Use CC-CV charging: constant current until reaching 3.8V, then constant voltage until current tapers to 0.05C. Never trickle charge or maintain at full voltage continuously - this accelerates degradation. Charge temperature range is 0°C to 45°C; charging below 0°C causes lithium plating. Do not discharge below 2.2V - deep discharge can damage the cell. Pre-charging is not required - hybrid capacitors can be charged from 0V. Simple resistor-limited charging is not recommended due to voltage overshoot risk. Use dedicated LIC charger ICs or voltage-limited supplies with accurate regulation (±50mV).
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use a dedicated charger IC or precision voltage source with current limiting. Contact our FAE team for recommended charging circuits.

faqs.question: What is the expected cycle life of hybrid capacitors?
faqs.answer: Aowei hybrid capacitors are rated for 50,000 to 100,000 charge/discharge cycles depending on operating conditions. This is significantly less than EDLC (500,000+ cycles) but far exceeds lithium batteries (500-2000 cycles). Cycle life is defined as the number of cycles before capacitance degrades below 80% of initial value when operated within specified parameters. Factors affecting cycle life: Depth of discharge - shallow cycles (20-30% DOD) can achieve 100,000+ cycles, while deep cycles (80-100% DOD) yield 20,000-30,000 cycles. Charge voltage - operating at 3.6V instead of 3.8V can double cycle life. Charge current - lower C-rates extend life; 0.5C charging achieves better life than 1C. Temperature - operating and charging at 25°C provides optimal life; 45°C operation reduces life by approximately 50%. For maximum cycle life, use shallow discharge cycles, 3.6V charge voltage, and 0.5C charge current.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring maximum cycle life, limit depth of discharge to 50% and charge voltage to 3.6V.

faqs.question: Can hybrid capacitors replace batteries in my application?
faqs.answer: Hybrid capacitors can replace batteries in specific applications but are not universal replacements. Suitable applications have: Frequent charging opportunities (daily or more), moderate energy requirements (minutes to hours of runtime), need for wide temperature operation, requirement for 10+ year service life without replacement, burst power requirements with standby periods, or safety concerns with lithium batteries. Unsuitable applications include: Long runtime requirements (days between charging), very high continuous power draw, cost-sensitive applications where battery cost is critical, or applications requiring maximum energy density. When evaluating replacement, compare: Energy density (hybrid: 20-30 Wh/kg vs Li-ion: 150-250 Wh/kg), Cycle life (hybrid: 50,000 vs Li-ion: 500-2000), Temperature range (hybrid: -40°C to +65°C vs Li-ion: 0°C to +45°C), Self-discharge (hybrid: 5-10%/day vs Li-ion: 1-2%/month), and Safety (hybrid: non-flammable vs Li-ion: thermal runaway risk). Many applications benefit from hybrid-battery combinations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for a detailed comparison analysis specific to your application requirements.
